message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Johannes (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 700+ reviews (511 photos, 704 reviews per search snippet); TripAdvisor 667 reviews; OpenTable cited 4,278 reviews (high volume across platforms)
- Sentiment: mostly positive with emerging mixed signals
- Longevity: Opened late October 1999; celebrated 25 years in 2024; continuous chef/owner stability under Johannes Bacher
- Credentials: ["OpenTable Diners' Choice Award 2025","Yelp 'People Love You' Award 2023","Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice — Best Continental Restaurant, Best Romantic Dining, Best Martini (multi-year)","Featured in Mimi Sheraton's '1,000 Foods to Eat Before You Die' (Wiener Schnitzel)","Featured in 'Best Chefs America' (inaugural edition) — Chef Johannes Bacher","2022 Palm Springs Business Hall of Fame recognition","Zagat-rated; press in The New York Times, Bon Appetit, Wine Spectator, Travel & Leisure, Desert Sun, Palm Springs Life"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Austrian Wiener Schnitzel Palm Springs/Coachella Valley' (dominant), 'Modern European Palm Springs' (top-3 with Miro's and Europa), 'date night/romantic Palm Springs' (cited but not the headline pick — 4 Saints, Copley's, Le Vallauris lead), 'fine dining downtown Palm Springs' (mentioned but does NOT lead 2026 roundups, which favor 4 Saints, Bar Issi, Copley's, Maleza). Largely absent from broad 'best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026' lists which lean toward Le Vallauris, Pacifica, Edge Steakhouse, Copley's.
- Site structured data: WordPress site at johannespalmsprings.com. No JSON-LD or schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness/Menu markup detected in HTML. Hours, NAP (196 S. Indian Canyon Dr, (760) 778-0017), and Google Maps embed are present as plain text. Menu is image-heavy without machine-readable structure. Strong human-readable content but weak agent-readable structured data — significant gap for LLM/SERP knowledge panels.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

**1) JSON-LD Schema Tags:**
No JSON-LD script tags (application/ld+json) are present in the provided HTML.

**2) Schema.org Microdata:**

**3) Contact Information Listed:**

**4) Menu Format:**
The menu appears image-based rather than structured HTML text. The content displays dish photos (scallops, chocolate mousse, cocktails, duck, schnitzel) with captions but no detailed HTML menu markup.

**5) CMS/Platform:**
**WordPress** is confirmed. Evidence includes WordPress-specific URL patterns ("wp-content/uploads"), and the footer credits "Design & SEO by Optimize4youSEO," a WordPress-focused service provider.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Johannes Restaurant in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Chef Johannes Bacher's global modern European award-winning cuisine, including his popular "Wiener Schnitzel," has been a downtown Palm Springs staple since 1999. Johannes Bacher was born and raised in a small mountain village in Tirol, Austria.

## The Wiener Schnitzel
The Schnitzel is described as sublime with attentive but not intrusive service and tables with respectable spacing. It was actually the schnitzel-loving locals who prompted Austria-born chef Johannes Bacher to start offering the dish at his eponymous Palm Springs restaurant.

Bacher tenderizes the veal down to an eighth of an inch, then seasons it with salt and pepper, dips it in flour and egg wash, and coats it with the restaurant's proprietary bread-crumb mixture—a combination of panko and pretzels. After that, he dips the cutlets in the deep fryer for one minute before pan-sautéeing them in bubbling butter.

## Menu and Variations
Since making that first plate at his restaurant by request, Bacher's schnitzel offerings have ballooned to include a full menu of variations, with one coming with a green Madagascan peppercorn sauce, another with a lemon-caper-parsley-butter sauce, and the pizza-inspired "Mama's" style topped with tomato, cheese, and arugula.

## Location and Details
The restaurant is located on the corner of South Indian Canyon and Arenas Road. The restaurant is located at 196 S Indian Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining options in downtown Palm Springs for 2026:

## Michelin-Recognized Restaurants

4 Saints is a rooftop dining room at the Kimpton Rowan Palm Springs that feels lively with indoor and outdoor seating and wide-open views, featuring a four-course prix fixe and à la carte menu with standouts like wagyu steak frites and seared scallops.

## Hotel Dining Destinations

The Thompson Palm Springs opened in 2024 in the heart of downtown with two onsite restaurants, including Lola Rose Grand Mezze with a Mediterranean menu offering shakshuka and shareable mezze like spiced lamb kebab and saffron chicken.

Bar Issi at the Thompson features a coastal Italian menu with wood-fired pizzas and housemade pastas, with a strong cocktail program that makes reservations hard to come by.

## Classic & Historic Establishments

Copley's on Palm Canyon, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate, made OpenTable's "100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America" list and offers stunning mountain views from the patio.

Mr. Lyons is a historic steakhouse that has been around since the 1940s and after a 2015 renovation retaken its place as the desert's go-to spot for martinis and giant slabs of meat.

## Contemporary Options

Alice B. brings elegant Californian/Mediterranean fusion to downtown with a refined yet welcoming vibe and indoor and patio seating for dinner or weekend brunch.

Palm Canyon Drive is where Palm Springs fine dining comes alive, with 4 Saints offering a dining experience from its rooftop location in downtown Palm Springs.

Based on the web search results, here are some of the best restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2026:

## Fine Dining Options

The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine.

Pacifica Seafood Restaurant in Palm Desert is known for its fresh catches and extensive wine list, offering a sophisticated yet relaxed dining experience with a range of seafood options from oysters to Chilean sea bass.

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage specializes in prime steaks and fresh seafood, with floor-to-ceiling windows providing a breathtaking backdrop for a memorable dining experience.

Copley's On Palm Canyon is housed in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate and offers a mix of American and European cuisine, focusing on seasonal ingredients with al fresco dining in the charming courtyard.

Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers a refined farm-to-table dining experience inspired by the flavors of the American Southwest.

## Casual & Specialty Dining

Jackalope Ranch is a popular restaurant and event venue located in Indio, known for its rustic and lively atmosphere offering a diverse menu featuring Southwestern and American cuisine with beautiful desert surroundings.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Johannes in Palm Springs as a romantic date night restaurant:

## Johannes Palm Springs - Romantic Restaurant Information

Chef Johannes Bacher's award-winning European cuisine has been satisfying discerning taste buds in downtown Palm Springs for more than two decades. Johannes is a popular choice for date night thanks to consistently praised, creative modern-European cuisine, a warm dining room and a well-crafted cocktail and wine list, with the restaurant's cozy main dining room and patio creating a welcoming, slightly upscale-casual atmosphere.

### Restaurant Details:
- **Location:** 196 S. Indian Canyon Drive, Palm Springs, CA 92262
- **Hours:** Wednesday – Sunday, 5 PM – 9 PM; Closed Monday & Tuesday
- **Contact:** Phone: (760) 778-0017 or reservations through OpenTable

### What to Expect:
The restaurant has been voted Best Continental Restaurant, Best Martini, and Best Romantic Dining by readers of Palm Springs Life. Signature dishes include cheese spätzle and his signature wiener schnitzel, which was featured in 1,000 Foods to Eat Before You Die. The upscale yet relaxed dining experience pairs with a comprehensive wine list, featuring half-bottles as well as 3-ounce tasting pours, and trendy, Euro-inspired décor.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the best romantic Palm Springs restaurants you mentioned:

## Le Vallauris

Le Vallauris Restaurant is a refined dining destination in Palm Springs, offering a prix-fixe French-focused menu and a romantic patio setting. Housed in a 1927 Mediterranean revival home, this classic restaurant has been a local favorite for over four decades. The outdoor setting under century-old banyan trees, paired with warm ambient lighting, creates a truly enchanting atmosphere. Expect an elegant, old-school Palm Springs ambiance, romantic tree-lit patio dining, classic white-tablecloth service, live guitar or piano at times, and a genteel interior in a historic house.

## Copley's on Palm Canyon

Copley's on Palm Canyon, located in Uptown Palm Springs and part of the former Cary Grant estate, is a romantic restaurant known for its elevated American dishes. The celebrity chef Andrew Manion Copley brings his expertise to the sublime cuisine, which includes options like Muscovy duck breast, seared Scottish salmon, lobster pot pie, and tandoori chicken breast. Diners can't get enough of the romantic, open-air patio in the garden.

## 4 Saints

4 Saints - Located on the seventh-floor rooftop of the Kimpton Rowan Hotel, serving modern American coastal cuisine with stunning San Jacinto Mountain views and dishes showcasing seafood, local produce, and prime meats.

All three restaurants are highly recommended for romantic dinners and special occasions in Palm Springs!

content.text: I have enough to verify the claims now. Let me compile findings.

confirms: Yelp review counts (511 photos, 704 reviews) confirmed verbatim in Yelp page title for johannes-palm-springs-2
TripAdvisor 667 reviews confirmed (4.3 stars, ranked #36 of 310 in Palm Springs)
OpenTable Diners' Choice Award 2025 confirmed (dedicated page on johannespalmsprings.com and search snippet)
Longevity since 1999 / 25-year anniversary confirmed via Palm Springs Tribune article 'Johannes Celebrates A Quarter Of A Century In Palm Springs' (Oct 29, 2024)
Chef/owner Johannes Bacher continuous stewardship confirmed; Austrian (Tirol) origin confirmed
Wiener Schnitzel featured in Mimi Sheraton's '1,000 Foods to Eat Before You Die' confirmed in multiple sources
Featured in 'Best Chefs America' (inaugural edition) confirmed via Palm Springs Life article
2022 Palm Springs Business Hall of Fame qualification confirmed (after 3 consecutive Best of Palm Springs European Cuisine wins)
Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice for Best Continental, Best Martini, Best Romantic Dining confirmed
Address 196 S Indian Canyon Dr and phone (760) 778-0017 confirmed on website source
WordPress platform confirmed (wp-content/uploads path, Optimize4youSEO footer credit)
No JSON-LD application/ld+json script tags confirmed; no schema.org microdata (itemscope/itemtype) confirmed
Menu is image-based without machine-readable HTML structure — confirmed
Surfaces dominantly for 'Austrian Wiener Schnitzel Palm Springs' queries — confirmed (top results)

Does NOT lead 2026 'best fine dining downtown Palm Springs' roundups — confirmed; lists lead with 4 Saints, Bar Issi, Copley's, Alice B., Mr. Lyons; Johannes absent from Spirit of Sofia 2026 downtown list summary and Infatuation top-25 summary
Largely absent from broad 'best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026' lists — confirmed; those favor Pacifica, Edge Steakhouse, Copley's, Morgan's, Purple Room
contradicts: OpenTable review count claim cites '4,278 reviews' — the current OpenTable figure surfaced in search is 4,031 reviews at 4.8 stars. Still 'high volume' but the specific 4,278 number is not the current count (may have been at a different snapshot). Minor numerical drift, not a category error.
flagged_as_unverified: Yelp 'People Love You' Award 2023 — could not independently surface this specific award in search results
Zagat-rated claim — not independently verified in this pass (Zagat as a brand has been dormant since ~2020; possibly legacy)
Specific press placements in The New York Times, Bon Appetit, Wine Spectator, Travel & Leisure — not independently verified individually in this pass (Desert Sun and Palm Springs Life confirmed)

Sentiment characterization 'mostly positive with emerging mixed signals' — not directly tested via review-text sampling in this pass; TripAdvisor 4.3 and OpenTable 4.8 are consistent with mostly-positive, but 'emerging mixed signals' is a qualitative judgment unverified here
Visibility gap score of 6 — subjective composite, not externally verifiable, but consistent with confirmed structured-data absence + strong-but-not-leading SERP presence
Claim that 'Modern European Palm Springs' query places Johannes top-3 with Miro's and Europa — not directly tested in this pass
input.notes: Assessment is largely accurate. Structured-data gap (no JSON-LD, no schema.org microdata, image-based menu, WordPress) independently confirmed by fetching site source. Awards/longevity/SERP positioning hold up: Johannes dominates Wiener Schnitzel queries but is genuinely absent from 2026 'best downtown Palm Springs' and 'best Coachella Valley' headline roundups, which lead with 4 Saints, Bar Issi, Copley's, Le Vallauris, Pacifica, Edge. Only meaningful discrepancy: OpenTable review count is currently ~4,031 (not 4,278). Yelp 'People Love You' 2023 award, Zagat rating, and several national-press placements (NYT, Bon Appetit, Wine Spectator, T&L) could not be independently confirmed in this pass and should be flagged. Convertibility tier 'convertible' is well supported — adding LocalBusiness/Restaurant/Menu JSON-LD to a stable WordPress site is a low-friction fix.
